title.purpose: Native large-title headers; platform-rendered, never hand-rolled.

headline.purpose: Person names in cards, large button text.

body.purpose: Fact values, notes, row content. 1rem = 14px (Uniwind rem polyfill).

label.purpose: Caption above a fact value; Ink Muted, sentence case.

shadows.name: whisper
shadows.purpose: The only shadow. Warm-tinted; applied to cards sitting on Paper.

motion.name: pressed-state
motion.purpose: Instant, plain pressed feedback. No springs, no scale, no choreography.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Calm ink fill for primary actions — deliberately not amber.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #18181B; color: #FAFAFA; height: 48px; padding: 12px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 5px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; transition: opacity 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:active { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #18181B; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Delete confirmations; the only non-amber saturated color.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { background: #EF4444; color: #FAFAFA; height: 48px; padding: 12px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 5px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; transition: opacity 0.15s; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-destructive:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EF4444; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Person Card
components.description: List card: Cream Swirl avatar, name, visible warm-gray chevron, whisper shadow on Paper.
components.html: AAnna Petrova
components.css: .ds-person-card { display: flex; align-items: center; background: #FFFFFF; border-radius: 14px; padding: 14px; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(28, 20, 8, 0.06); font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-person-card:hover { background: #FCFAF7; } .ds-avatar { width: 48px; height: 48px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #FBEAC9; color: #B07818; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; margin-right: 14px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-person-name { font-size: 16px; font-weight: 500; color: #09090B; flex: 1; } .ds-chevron { flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Entry Row
components.description: Fact row: optional muted label over a prominent value; tap to edit and swipe to delete.
components.html: Favorite coffeeFlat white, oat milk
components.css: .ds-entry-row { display: flex; flex-direction: column; background: #FFFFFF; padding: 10.5px 14px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s; } .ds-entry-row:hover { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05); } .ds-entry-row:active { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05); } .ds-entry-row:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #18181B; outline-offset: -2px; } .ds-entry-label { font-size: 12.25px; color: #71717A; margin-bottom: 1.75px; } .ds-entry-value { font-size: 15.75px; color: #09090B; }

components.name: Date Row
components.description: Timeline-derived date row: day over month, prominent label, and optional year-plus-age detail; tap to edit and swipe to delete.
components.css: .ds-date-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10.5px; background: #FFFFFF; padding: 10.5px 14px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; color: #09090B; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s; } .ds-date-row:hover, .ds-date-row:active { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05); } .ds-date-row:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #18181B; outline-offset: -2px; } .ds-date-block { width: 38.5px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; } .ds-date-block strong, .ds-date-copy strong { font-size: 15.75px; line-height: 1.4; font-weight: 600; } .ds-date-block small { font-size: 12.25px; color: #71717A; } .ds-date-copy { display: flex; flex-direction: column; flex: 1; } .ds-date-copy strong { font-weight: 500; } .ds-date-copy small { margin-top: 1.75px; font-size: 14px; color: #71717A; }

components.name: Add Row
components.description: Card foot action: Noodle Gold plus icon with Broth label — the Golden Thread.
components.html: Add fact
components.css: .ds-add-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; background: #FFFFFF; padding: 10px 14px; border-top: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05); font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: #B07818; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-add-row:hover { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05); } .ds-add-row:active { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05); }

components.name: Input
components.description: Text field: white fill, hairline border, quiet focus.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #FFFFFF; color: #09090B; border: 1px solid #E4E4E7; border-radius: 5px; height: 48px; padding: 0 10px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #71717A; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #18181B; outline-offset: 1px; } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; }

narrative.overview: Lapsha looks like a notebook someone actually keeps: quiet warm paper, tidy white cards, and one golden thread of amber marking identity and accent actions. The content — people and the details you've saved about them — is intimate, so the interface stays out of the way. Chrome is native wherever the platform provides it, and the custom layer on top is deliberately plain: white cards on paper, hairline dividers, system type. This system explicitly rejects the sales-CRM look, the generic web-app-in-a-wrapper feel, and social-network profile aesthetics.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper background with white cards; separation by tone, not lines or heavy shadows
One amber family (Noodle Gold / Broth / Cream Swirl) carries warmth, identity, and accent interactions
System fonts only; the platform's own voice
Continuous-curve corners on every card — iOS-squircle softness
Density is low; every screen has one job

rules.name: The Golden Thread Rule
rules.body: Amber appears only on things that are interactive or identity-bearing (add actions, tints, avatars, selection). Amber as decoration — backgrounds, dividers, flourishes — is forbidden.

rules.name: The Paper Rule
rules.body: Screens sit on Paper (#F9F7F4); pure white is earned by being a card. Never place a screen on #FFFFFF and never place a card on a card.

rules.name: The System Voice Rule
rules.body: No custom or display fonts, ever. If a screen needs more hierarchy, use weight and the tonal Paper/White step, not a new typeface.

rules.name: The Whisper Rule
rules.body: One shadow token exists. If a surface needs more separation than Whisper provides, the answer is layout (spacing, tonal grouping), never a bigger shadow. If it looks lifted, it's wrong.

dos: Do keep every screen on Paper (#F9F7F4) with Card White groups — the tonal step is the layout system.
Do route all interactivity signaling through the amber family: Noodle Gold for icons/tints, Broth for accent text, Cream Swirl for soft fills.
Do use platform components first (sheets, pickers, swipe actions, large titles); reach for custom chrome only when the platform has no answer — and then keep it quiet.
Do use borderCurve: 'continuous' on every rounded card.
Do keep body text at full Ink (#09090B); labels may be Ink Muted, values never are.
Do keep pressed states instant and plain: opacity or black/5, 150–250ms, nothing choreographed.
donts: Don't let Lapsha look like a sales CRM (Salesforce, HubSpot) — no data tables of people, no pipelines, no stat dashboards about relationships.
Don't ship generic cross-platform chrome — no web-app-in-a-wrapper components, no identical custom UI on both platforms where native affordances differ.
Don't style person screens like social network profiles — no cover photos, follower-style counts, or public-profile layouts; these are private notes.
Don't use Noodle Gold (#F6B756) as text on any surface — it fails contrast everywhere; Broth is the only text amber.
Don't add a second shadow token, darken Whisper, or lift elements on press. If it looks elevated, it's too much.
Don't introduce custom fonts, uppercase-tracked labels, or decorative color. Warmth is amber + copy, never ornament.
Don't nest cards or put white on white — one Paper, one card layer, done.
